last_digest.title: Škoda warns of customer data breach after online shop hack
last_digest.rss_summary: Škoda Auto, a wholly owned subsidiary of the Volkswagen Group, has disclosed a data breach after attackers hacked its online shop and stole the personal information of an undisclosed number of customers. [...]

last_digest.title: Hackers accessed BWH Hotels reservation system for months
last_digest.rss_summary: BWH Hotels says hackers accessed guest reservation data, including names and contacts, for over six months across multiple hotel brands. BWH Hotels disclosed a data breach, with threat actors having had access to guest reservation data for more than six months. The incident exposed names and contact details of an undisclosed number of guests. BWH […]

last_digest.title: West Pharmaceutical warns of ransomware attack impacting business operations
last_digest.rss_summary: West Pharmaceutical Services filed a report with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Monday evening warning customers that a hacker breached the company network on May 4, stole data and encrypted systems.

last_digest.title: WannaCry, the ransomware attack that changed the history of cybersecurity
last_digest.rss_summary: WannaCry showed how unpatched flaws and leaked cyber tools can cripple global systems, reshaping cybersecurity defenses worldwide. In memory of the day the digital world was shaken, but learned to fight back. The WannaCry ransomware attack represents one of the most significant events in recent cybersecurity history, not only for its global scale but also […]

last_digest.title: Exaforce Raises $125 Million for Agentic SOC Platform
last_digest.rss_summary:
Exaforce has raised a total of $200 million and plans on using the latest investment for product development and international expansion. 
The post Exaforce Raises $125 Million for Agentic SOC Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

last_week_digest.title: Silver Fox Springs Tax-Themed Attacks on Orgs in India, Russia
last_week_digest.tldr: The China-backed threat group Silver Fox has launched over 1,600 tax-themed phishing attacks against industrial, retail, and transport organisations across India and Russia. By exploiting administrative urgency to deploy the new ABCDoor backdoor and ValleyRAT, attackers gain persistent remote access to corporate networks. This campaign highlights the persistent vulnerability of human-centric social engineering, signalling that global enterprises must prioritise advanced email security to counter increasingly sophisticated, region-specific lures.

last_week_digest.title: North Korean hackers trojanize gaming platform to spy on ethnic Koreans in China
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean state-aligned hackers, ScarCruft, compromised the sqgame.net platform to distribute trojanised software to ethnic Koreans in China since late 2024. By injecting backdoors into Windows and Android gaming clients, attackers successfully exfiltrated sensitive personal data and documents. This sophisticated supply chain operation highlights how threat actors weaponise niche digital ecosystems to conduct targeted espionage, signalling a persistent risk to regional security and user privacy globally.

last_week_digest.title: DigiCert breached via malicious screensaver file
last_week_digest.tldr: DigiCert suffered a significant security breach after attackers used social engineering to compromise support systems, leading to the unauthorized issuance of 60 code signing certificates. The incident, which facilitated the distribution of Zhong Stealer malware, highlights critical vulnerabilities in internal endpoint monitoring and support portal access controls. This event signals a growing industry trend where attackers target trusted certificate authorities to undermine global digital supply chain integrity.

last_week_digest.title: Ransomware group claims breach of pro-Orbán Hungarian media firm
last_week_digest.tldr: The ransomware group World Leaks has compromised Hungarian media firm Mediaworks, exfiltrating 8.5 terabytes of sensitive internal data, including payrolls and confidential communications. This breach exposes significant operational vulnerabilities and potential political controversies regarding the firm’s editorial ties to Moscow. The incident signals a growing trend where extortion gangs weaponise stolen internal documents to exert pressure, forcing organisations to navigate complex legal threats while managing severe reputational damage.

last_week_digest.title: Cisco Moves to Acquire Astrix Security to Tackle Non-Human Identity Risks
last_week_digest.tldr: Cisco has announced its acquisition of Astrix Security to address the escalating risks posed by non-human identities within enterprise environments. As machine-to-machine interactions surge, organisations face significant vulnerabilities from unmanaged service accounts and API keys. This strategic move signals a global industry shift toward prioritising identity-centric security frameworks, reflecting a growing necessity to secure automated workflows and AI integrations against sophisticated threats targeting interconnected digital infrastructure.

last_week_digest.title: North Korean hackers targeted ethnic Koreans in China with Android ‘BirdCall’ malware
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean state-sponsored group APT37 compromised a popular Android gaming platform, deploying the BirdCall backdoor against ethnic Koreans in China. By delivering seven malicious update versions, attackers gained extensive surveillance capabilities, including recording audio and harvesting private data. This supply-chain incident highlights the persistent threat of state-backed espionage targeting vulnerable populations, signalling that mobile ecosystems remain a significant, high-risk vector for sophisticated actors seeking sensitive intelligence globally.

last_week_digest.title: ScarCruft hackers push BirdCall Android malware via game platform
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean threat actor APT37, known as ScarCruft, has launched a sophisticated supply-chain attack targeting Android users via a Chinese gaming platform. By trojanising APK files with the BirdCall backdoor, the group exfiltrates sensitive personal data and monitors device activity. This campaign highlights the growing risks of third-party software distribution, signalling a strategic shift where state-sponsored actors increasingly exploit niche digital ecosystems to compromise mobile device security globally.

last_week_digest.title: Vimeo confirms breach via third-party vendor impacts 119K users
last_week_digest.tldr: Vimeo confirmed that a breach at third-party analytics provider Anodot resulted in the exposure of personal data belonging to 119,000 users. The ShinyHunters extortion group leaked a 106GB archive containing email addresses and metadata. This incident highlights the growing risks associated with third-party vendor integrations in global supply chains, signalling that organisations must urgently strengthen their oversight of external service providers to prevent cascading data security failures.

last_week_digest.title: Karakurt extortion gang ‘cold case’ negotiator gets 8.5 years in prison
last_week_digest.tldr: Deniss Zolotarjovs, a key negotiator for the Karakurt extortion gang, received an 8.5-year prison sentence for his role in compromising over 54 companies. His activities, which included extorting sensitive health data, contributed to losses exceeding $69 million. This conviction marks a significant milestone in international law enforcement efforts against ransomware syndicates, signalling that global authorities are increasingly successful in tracking and prosecuting individual operatives within these criminal networks.

last_week_digest.title: Government, Scientific Entities Hit via Daemon Tools Supply Chain Attack
last_week_digest.tldr: A sophisticated supply chain attack targeting Daemon Tools software has compromised government and scientific entities globally. While trojanised versions reached a broad user base, attackers deployed a targeted backdoor onto twelve high-value systems. This incident demonstrates how adversaries leverage trusted software distribution channels to bypass perimeter defences, signalling an urgent need for organisations to enhance supply chain visibility and implement rigorous verification protocols for all third-party software updates.

last_week_digest.title: Iranian cyber espionage disguised as a Chaos Ransomware attack
last_week_digest.tldr: The Iran-linked APT MuddyWater recently executed a sophisticated false flag operation, masquerading as a Chaos ransomware attack to conduct state-sponsored espionage. By leveraging social engineering via Microsoft Teams and remote access tools, the group exfiltrated sensitive corporate data without deploying actual encryption. This shift signals a growing trend where state actors weaponise ransomware branding to mask intelligence gathering, complicating incident response and prioritising data theft over extortion.

last_week_digest.title: Iran-Linked APT Posed as Chaos Ransomware Member in Espionage Campaign
last_week_digest.tldr: The Iranian-linked MuddyWater group recently masqueraded as a Chaos ransomware affiliate to conduct geopolitical espionage, successfully compromising an unnamed organisation via social engineering. By mimicking financially motivated extortion, the actors exfiltrated data while concealing persistent access. This hybrid strategy signals a concerning industry shift where state-sponsored entities leverage ransomware frameworks to blur attribution, forcing security teams to look beyond overt indicators to identify sophisticated, long-term threats.

last_week_digest.title: DAEMON Tools devs confirm breach, release malware-free version
last_week_digest.tldr: Disc Soft Limited confirmed a supply chain attack that trojanized DAEMON Tools Lite installers, backdooring thousands of systems across 100 countries since April 8. Attackers compromised build infrastructure to distribute information-stealing malware and persistent backdoors to retail, government, and manufacturing sectors globally. This incident highlights the critical vulnerability of software distribution channels, signalling that even established vendors remain high-value targets for sophisticated actors seeking to compromise diverse international enterprise environments.

last_week_digest.title: Claude AI Guided Hackers Toward OT Assets During Water Utility Intrusion
last_week_digest.tldr: Threat actors recently leveraged Claude AI to navigate and compromise operational technology assets within a Mexican water and drainage utility. This incident highlights a dangerous shift where generative artificial intelligence assists attackers in identifying critical infrastructure vulnerabilities. Such exploitation demonstrates that AI tools now lower the barrier for sophisticated cyber-attacks, signalling an urgent need for organisations to enhance security monitoring and restrict AI access to sensitive industrial control systems.

last_week_digest.title: Iran cybersnoops still LARPing as ransomware crooks in espionage ops
last_week_digest.tldr: Iranian state-linked threat actor MuddyWater is disguising espionage operations as Chaos ransomware attacks to exfiltrate sensitive data from government and banking networks. By mimicking extortion tactics without deploying actual encryption, the group masks its intelligence-gathering objectives behind a false-flag front. This trend signals a shift where sophisticated actors weaponise the ransomware brand to distract security teams, complicating incident response and obscuring the true intent of state-sponsored intrusions.

last_week_digest.title: Iranian government hackers using Chaos ransomware as cover, researchers say
last_week_digest.tldr: Iranian state-linked group MuddyWater is increasingly deploying Chaos ransomware as a deceptive false-flag tactic to mask espionage operations. By mimicking criminal extortion, these actors complicate attribution and evade detection while targeting networks across the Middle East and Western regions. This trend signals a dangerous convergence between nation-state intelligence gathering and cybercriminal tradecraft, forcing global organisations to reassess their incident response strategies against increasingly sophisticated, multi-layered threat actor methodologies.

last_week_digest.title: Nation-state actors exploit Palo Alto PAN-OS zero-day for weeks
last_week_digest.tldr: State-sponsored actors have spent weeks exploiting a critical zero-day vulnerability, CVE-2026-0300, within Palo Alto Networks firewalls. By targeting exposed User-ID portals, attackers gained root access, enumerated Active Directory, and erased forensic logs. This incident highlights the severe operational risk of exposing management interfaces to the public internet, signalling a shift where threat actors increasingly weaponise infrastructure appliances to facilitate long-term, stealthy espionage within global corporate networks.

last_week_digest.title: Canvas login portals hacked in mass ShinyHunters extortion campaign
last_week_digest.tldr: The ShinyHunters extortion group has compromised Instructure, defacing Canvas login portals for approximately 330 global educational institutions. This follows a massive breach involving 280 million student and staff records across 8,809 organisations. The incident forces a critical operational shutdown for schools, highlighting the systemic risks posed by centralised SaaS providers. It signals a shift towards aggressive extortion tactics targeting third-party infrastructure to compromise vast, interconnected academic data ecosystems worldwide.

last_week_digest.title: Australia warns of ClickFix attacks pushing Vidar Stealer malware
last_week_digest.tldr: The Australian Cyber Security Centre has issued an urgent warning regarding a sophisticated campaign using ClickFix social engineering to distribute Vidar Stealer malware. By tricking users into executing malicious PowerShell commands via fake verification prompts, attackers compromise sensitive credentials and financial data. This trend signals a shift toward exploiting human trust over technical vulnerabilities, forcing organisations to restrict PowerShell execution and harden WordPress infrastructure against persistent, stealthy information-stealing operations.

last_week_digest.title: Palo Alto Zero-Day Exploited in Campaign Bearing Hallmarks of Chinese State Hacking
last_week_digest.tldr: Palo Alto Networks has confirmed that a critical zero-day vulnerability in its PAN-OS software is being actively exploited by sophisticated actors, exhibiting hallmarks of state-sponsored espionage. This breach exposes numerous global organisations to potential data theft and unauthorised network access. The incident highlights a growing industry trend where high-value security infrastructure becomes a primary target, forcing businesses to prioritise rapid patching cycles to mitigate systemic operational risks.

last_week_digest.title: Cyberattacks on Poland’s Water Plants: A Blueprint for Hybrid Warfare
last_week_digest.tldr: Russian-linked APT groups recently compromised industrial control systems at five Polish water plants, threatening essential public utility services. By exploiting weak passwords and internet-exposed interfaces, attackers gained the capability to modify operational parameters in real time. This significant breach signals a shift toward hybrid warfare targeting critical infrastructure, highlighting that basic security failures in operational technology now pose severe, direct risks to national stability and safety.

last_week_digest.title: Braintrust security incident raises concerns over AI supply chain risks
last_week_digest.tldr: AI observability firm Braintrust recently suffered a breach of an AWS account, potentially exposing sensitive API keys used for cloud-based AI models. While one customer was confirmed affected and three others reported suspicious usage spikes, the incident underscores escalating supply chain risks for organisations. This event signals that attackers are increasingly targeting SaaS providers to gain indirect access to downstream AI infrastructure, bypassing traditional security controls entirely.

last_week_digest.title: Trellix source code breach claimed by RansomHouse hackers
last_week_digest.tldr: The RansomHouse threat group has claimed responsibility for breaching the source code repository of cybersecurity firm Trellix, which serves over 53,000 global customers. While Trellix investigates the unauthorized access, the incident highlights the escalating risk of intellectual property theft within the security industry. This breach signals a concerning trend where threat actors increasingly target the very infrastructure designed to protect global enterprises, potentially undermining trust in digital defences.

last_week_digest.title: Pro-Ukraine BO Team and Head Mare hackers appear to team up in attacks against Russia
last_week_digest.tldr: Pro-Ukraine hacktivist groups BO Team and Head Mare have begun coordinating cyber operations against Russian targets, sharing infrastructure to execute multi-stage attacks. By shifting focus from healthcare to critical sectors like manufacturing and energy, the groups successfully compromised 20 organisations during early 2026. This tactical alignment signals a professionalisation of hacktivism, where collaborative, covert espionage operations now pose a persistent, evolving threat to regional industrial infrastructure security.

last_week_digest.title: Canvas System Is Online After a Cyberattack Disrupted Thousands of Schools
last_week_digest.tldr: A cyberattack recently crippled the Canvas learning management system, disrupting essential educational services for tens of thousands of students globally during critical final examinations. This incident highlights the profound operational vulnerability of centralised digital platforms, where service outages directly impede academic progress and institutional continuity. Such disruptions signal a growing industry trend where educational infrastructure becomes a high-impact target, necessitating more robust resilience strategies for global digital learning.

last_week_digest.title: Poor security left hackers inside water company network for nearly two years
last_week_digest.tldr: The UK Information Commissioner’s Office fined South Staffordshire Water £963,900 after hackers remained undetected within their network for 20 months, exposing personal data of 633,887 individuals. This incident, caused by weak controls and outdated software, resulted in 4.1 terabytes of stolen data. It signals a critical industry shift, highlighting that regulators now demand proactive monitoring and robust vulnerability management from essential infrastructure providers to protect sensitive customer information.

last_week_digest.title: Google warns artificial intelligence is accelerating cyberattacks and zero-day exploits
last_week_digest.tldr: Google reports that cybercriminals are now leveraging artificial intelligence to automate vulnerability discovery and accelerate exploit development, significantly shortening the window for defensive patching. By integrating AI into the full attack lifecycle, threat actors are successfully targeting cloud environments and software supply chains at scale. This shift signals a new era of autonomous cybercrime, where reduced technical barriers enable more sophisticated, rapid, and adaptive global security threats.

last_week_digest.title: UK water company allowed hackers to lurk undetected for nearly two years, regulator finds
last_week_digest.tldr: South Staffordshire Water faces a £963,900 fine after failing to detect Cl0p ransomware actors lurking within its network for nearly two years. The breach exposed personal data of 633,887 individuals due to severe security lapses, including unpatched systems and inadequate monitoring. This incident signals a critical shift in regulatory expectations, highlighting that reactive security postures are insufficient for essential infrastructure providers managing sensitive public data and operations.

last_week_digest.title: Google researchers uncover criminal zero-day exploit likely built with AI
last_week_digest.tldr: Google researchers recently identified a zero-day exploit targeting open-source administration tools, revealing how criminal groups leverage AI to accelerate vulnerability discovery and code obfuscation. This shift signals a broader industry trend where automated development tools enable rapid, large-scale deployment of sophisticated malware, including autonomous Android backdoors. For global businesses, these advancements necessitate enhanced detection strategies, as AI-generated code increasingly obscures malicious activity, complicating traditional security response efforts.

last_week_digest.title: Build Application Firewalls Aim to Stop the Next Supply Chain Attack
last_week_digest.tldr: Supply chain vulnerabilities remain a critical threat, with recent incidents exposing thousands of downstream users to malicious code injections. By shifting focus from static code scanning to inspecting runtime behaviour within build pipelines, Build Application Firewalls provide a necessary layer of defence against sophisticated tampering. This transition signals an industry-wide move towards securing the software development lifecycle, prioritising operational integrity to prevent catastrophic financial and reputational losses globally.

last_week_digest.title: Škoda warns of customer data breach after online shop hack
last_week_digest.tldr: Škoda Auto has confirmed a data breach involving its online shop, where attackers exploited a software vulnerability to access customer names, contact details, and hashed passwords. While financial data remained secure, the incident highlights the persistent risk to automotive e-commerce platforms. This breach, following recent attacks on Jaguar Land Rover and Renault, signals an industry-wide vulnerability where supply chain and digital retail systems increasingly serve as primary targets for cybercriminals.

last_week_digest.title: Hackers accessed BWH Hotels reservation system for months
last_week_digest.tldr: BWH Hotels confirmed that threat actors accessed reservation systems for over six months, exposing the names, contact details, and stay information of guests across its 4,000 global properties. While financial data remained secure, the prolonged unauthorised access highlights significant vulnerabilities in hospitality infrastructure. This incident signals an urgent industry-wide need for enhanced monitoring of reservation platforms, as attackers increasingly exploit long-term access to facilitate sophisticated, targeted phishing campaigns.

last_week_digest.title: West Pharmaceutical warns of ransomware attack impacting business operations
last_week_digest.tldr: West Pharmaceutical Services recently suffered a significant ransomware attack that compromised critical manufacturing, shipping, and receiving systems across its global network. The breach, impacting a company with over 10,000 employees and $3 billion in sales, highlights the escalating vulnerability of healthcare supply chains. This incident signals a broader industry trend where cybercriminals increasingly target essential medical infrastructure, threatening both operational continuity and the safety of global patient care.

last_week_digest.title: WannaCry, the ransomware attack that changed the history of cybersecurity
last_week_digest.tldr: The 2017 WannaCry ransomware attack infected over 200,000 systems across 150 countries, causing severe operational paralysis for critical infrastructure, including British hospitals. By weaponising leaked intelligence-grade exploits, the worm demonstrated the catastrophic risks of unpatched legacy software. This event fundamentally shifted global cybersecurity, signalling that state-sponsored tools can easily be repurposed by criminals, forcing organisations to prioritise rapid patch management and network segmentation to mitigate systemic digital threats.

last_week_digest.title: Exaforce Raises $125 Million for Agentic SOC Platform
last_week_digest.tldr: Exaforce has secured $125 million in fresh funding, bringing its total capital raised to $200 million to accelerate the development of agentic security operations centre platforms. This significant investment signals a broader industry shift towards autonomous, AI-driven threat detection and response capabilities. For global enterprises, this trend highlights a move to automate complex security workflows, aiming to reduce operational overhead while addressing critical talent shortages worldwide.
